---
title: "Article Title"
date: 2026-03-14
description: "One-sentence summary of the article."
toc: true
---

<div class="language-toggle" role="group" aria-label="Language switch"><a class="language-toggle__link is-active" href="/articles/article-slug/en.html" lang="en">EN</a><a class="language-toggle__link" href="/articles/article-slug/ko.html" lang="ko">KO</a></div>

## Problem
::: {.section-lead}
State the problem clearly in one sentence before expanding it.
:::

Add the minimum explanation needed to establish why the problem matters.

::: {.section-conclusion}
Use a short closing sentence when the section needs a clear takeaway.
:::

## Context
::: {.section-lead}
Add the minimum background needed for the reader to follow the article.
:::

::: {.symptom-block}
<p class="symptom-block__label">What breaks over time</p>

- Use a symptom block when the reader should quickly scan consequences or failure modes.
- Keep the label short and analytical.
:::

::: {.section-conclusion}
Close the section with a single sentence that names the real problem or takeaway.
:::

> Use a `blockquote` when you want to emphasize a claim, quote, or short framing sentence.

## Core Idea
::: {.section-lead}
Explain the central idea in plain language first.
:::

- Use a list when you need direct structure.
- Keep each bullet focused on one point.
- Convert repeated patterns into lists instead of dense paragraphs.

| Function | Tool |
|---|---|
| Search | Perplexity |
| Build | ChatGPT / Codex |

::: {.section-conclusion}
End the section by stating what the structure achieves, not just what it contains.
:::

## Principles
::: {.section-lead}
Use an ordered list when principles or steps need visible hierarchy.
:::

<ol class="ordered-list">
  <li>
    <span class="ordered-list__title">Lead with the principle</span>
    <span class="ordered-list__body">Follow with one or two sentences that explain why the principle matters.</span>
  </li>
  <li>
    <span class="ordered-list__title">Keep the title visually distinct</span>
    <span class="ordered-list__body">Use this component when a numbered sequence needs hierarchy rather than a flat procedural list.</span>
  </li>
</ol>

## Architecture
::: {.section-lead}
Use a diagram when the reader needs system structure, flow, or relationships.
:::

::: {.workflow-pipeline}
<span class="workflow-pipeline__step">Input</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__arrow" aria-hidden="true">&rarr;</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__step">Process</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__arrow" aria-hidden="true">&rarr;</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__step">Output</span>
:::

```{mermaid fig-cap="Replace with a caption that explains what the diagram shows."}
flowchart LR
  A[Input] --> B[Process]
  B --> C[Output]
```

## Structure
::: {.section-lead}
Use file-tree blocks for folder structures, pipeline layouts, or internal organization.
:::

<div class="file-tree" aria-label="Example project structure">
  <ul class="file-tree__root">
    <li class="file-tree__item">
      <div class="file-tree__folder is-open">
        <button class="file-tree__row file-tree__toggle" type="button" aria-expanded="true"><span class="file-tree__chevron" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__icon file-tree__icon--folder" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__label">Project</span></button>
        <ul class="file-tree__children">
          <li class="file-tree__item file-tree__item--folder"><div class="file-tree__row"><span class="file-tree__chevron-spacer" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__icon file-tree__icon--folder" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__label">app</span></div></li>
          <li class="file-tree__item file-tree__item--folder"><div class="file-tree__row"><span class="file-tree__chevron-spacer" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__icon file-tree__icon--folder" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__label">docs</span></div></li>
          <li class="file-tree__item file-tree__item--folder"><div class="file-tree__row"><span class="file-tree__chevron-spacer" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__icon file-tree__icon--folder" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__label">tests</span></div></li>
        </ul>
      </div>
    </li>
  </ul>
</div>

## Example
::: {.section-lead}
Use inline code like `gemini`, `gws`, or `librarian_memory` for exact terms.
:::

Use code blocks for runnable examples.

```python
from pathlib import Path

root = Path("AI_OS")
print(root.exists())
```

## References
::: {.section-lead}
Use a repository or reference card for links that deserve dedicated attention.
:::

::: {.repository-card}
[project-name](https://example.com)

Short explanation of why this repository, paper, or document matters.
:::

## Conclusion
::: {.section-lead}
Summarize the article in a few lines.
:::

End with the main takeaway, not a vague recap.
